Scarface's remake was a good idea because the original movie was about a bootlegger, and the remake instead made the smuggling operation a modern one where they were moving cocaine through Cuba, which made the plot more applicable to a new generation, which I don't hate.

I HATE being a picky eater dude
Yeah??????? Why dont people want to try bugs?????
Its literally the same shit as shellfish
I can't eat fish but I've tried eating 3 different kinds of sushi and each time it made me immediately vomit, each time more violently than the last
I want so badly to not be picky, but I just can't do most leafy greens, beans, or anything fish. I'll keep trying, I managed to make black licorice work, but each one is hard to pick up.
I once exchanged numbers with this girl and I asked over text what her hobbies were, and she said she didnt have any... HOW THE FUCK DO YOU NOT HAVE HOBBIES
I have so many tinder stories. One girl said she was into horror and all she liked was slasher jumpscare nonsense. And had the gaull to tell me my favourite movie (the thing) wasnt horror

I think being picky is something you can largely train yourself to get past, I managed to get to where I can drink kefir too. I didn't choose to have intense sensory issues, but refusing to confront them is absolutely a choice
My picky friend smonk and i have a deal. when we go out to eat, they can order the safe option but they have to try the thing I order
and theyre small so ill end up munching on their fries anyways

So there's a weird other side of this, because I was in Weird Kid class as a kid, and so they would basically train us, I'd say like dogs but honestly, no one treats dogs with that. They would basically punish us for avoiding uncomfortable stimuli and reward things that... hurt.

Going through that was super bad, but also prioritizing other people's convenience did lead to me being less picky and stuff. On the other hand, one time I was hiking and got a piece of glass in my shoe, and lacerated my foot all day with the glass because I didn't think it was ok to ask people to stop on my behalf

I couldn't see how avoiding inconveniencing people by asking them not to put brussel sprouts in my food was any different than inconveniencing them by asking them to stop so I could get glass out of my shoe
Anyway, the people subjecting me to that were doing that 25 years ago, and probably went to college for psychology 25 years before that. Half a century ago, psychology wasn't what it is today
